name: STM32 / CubeMX HAL
description: STMicroelectronics STM32 series (F4/F7/H7), Cortex-M single-core, native FreeRTOS
common_pitfalls: Native FreeRTOS: lower number = higher priority (opposite of ESP32/JL/BK), configMAX_PRIORITIES defaults to 56
HAL callbacks (HAL_I2S_RxCpltCallback etc.) run in ISR context, only FromISR API allowed
D-Cache coherency: DMA buffers must be in SRAM and 4-byte aligned; F7/H7 require SCB_CleanDCache/InvalidateDCache
Stack units are words (4 bytes/word), xTaskCreate parameter x 4 = bytes
H7 dual-core (Cortex-M7+M4) each have independent FreeRTOS instances, cross-core communication requires HSEM (hardware semaphore)
F4 has no hardware AES/SHA, WSS TLS uses software encryption/decryption, slow handshake with high stack usage
In STM32CubeMX generated code, osThreadNew is a CMSIS-RTOS2 wrapper, underlying implementation is still xTaskCreate
SysTick priority defaults to lowest (0xF), HAL_Delay depends on SysTick interrupt
STM32H7 AXI SRAM / SRAM1-4 have different address domains, placing DMA buffer in wrong domain causes HardFault
When F7/H7 D-Cache is enabled, camera/LCD/I2S DMA buffers must be clean/invalidate and cache-line aligned
When using LWIP + FreeRTOS, sys_now() needs to interface with HAL_GetTick

wss_tls.note: F4 pure software TLS handshake peak is high, H7 has hardware acceleration can reduce to 4096

lvgl.note: RGB LCD large screen needs more stack for DMA refresh

media_dma_buffer.note: D-Cache clean/invalidate + DMA SRAM frame pool

presenter.note: Presenter on STM32 is usually simple

pinmux_notes.PA13_PA14: SWD debug interface (SWDIO/SWCLK), can be released as GPIO before mass production
pinmux_notes.BOOT0: Boot mode selection, determines boot from Flash/System memory/SRAM at power-on
pinmux_notes.NRST: Reset pin, external circuit must have RC reset circuit
pinmux_notes.VBAT: Backup domain power supply, RTC/LSE powered by VBAT
